name: Honky Tonk Shepherd's Pie
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Chopped assorted wild mushrooms (oyster, portobello, shiitake)
recipeInstructions:
1. For the topping, place potatoes in a large saucepan of cold, salted water. Bring to boil and simmer partially covered for 20 minutes or until very tender. Drain.
2. Mash the potatoes in the pot until smooth. Add cream, butter, egg yolks, nutmeg, salt, and pepper to taste. Mash until smooth and fluffy. Keep warm.
3. For the filling, heat 1 tbsp oil and butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add onion, garlic, and chili flakes; cook for 2 minutes.
4. Add carrot, celery, thyme, salt, and pepper to taste; cook for 3 minutes. Set aside in a bowl.
5. Add another tbsp oil to the skillet and heat over medium-high heat. Add beef and lamb; cook, stirring continuously, until meat is no longer pink. Season with salt and pepper as desired. Discard thyme sprig and any excess fat from the pan.
6. Add vegetable mixture to the meat in the pan. Stir in stock and tomato paste. Reduce heat and cook covered on low for 15 minutes, stirring frequently.
7. Meanwhile, in a separate small frying pan, heat remaining oil over medium-high heat. Add mushrooms; cook for 2 minutes until soft.
8. Sprinkle mushrooms on the bottom of an ovenproof casserole dish (about 8 inches square).
9. Pour the meat mixture over the top, spreading evenly. Place tomato slices on the meat.
10. Pipe mashed potatoes over the meat mixture using a piping bag fitted with a star tip, or spread potatoes over the mixture.
11. Bake in a preheated 375°F oven for 25 to 30 minutes until potatoes are golden and the meat mixture is hot. If potatoes are not golden, place under broiler for 2 minutes before serving.
